title: U.S.-UK Financial Regulatory Working Group Winter 2026: Joint Statement
summary: The U.S.-UK Financial Regulatory Working Group held its 12th meeting on February 25, 2026, covering economic outlook, digital assets, AI in financial services, cybersecurity, non-bank financial intermediation (NBFI), and capital markets reforms including the UK's planned transition to T+1 settlement by October 2027.
key_points: Meeting included representatives from U.S. Treasury, HM Treasury, Federal Reserve, CFTC, FDIC, OCC, SEC, Bank of England, and FCA
Transatlantic Taskforce for Markets of the Future (TTMF) reported progress; second round of industry engagement held on capital markets links and digital assets
TTMF aims to deliver recommendations to both Treasuries by summer 2026
UK government committed to move to T+1 settlement cycle in October 2027
Participants discussed promoting digital assets and stablecoin adoption for payments in both jurisdictions
Both authorities discussed AI applications in financial services and ways to collaborate on potential risks
Working Group covers NBFI, banking regulation, cybersecurity, and operational resilience
why_it_matters: The UK T+1 settlement commitment (October 2027) represents a significant market structure change for UK capital markets. The TTMF aims to deliver recommendations on capital market links and digital assets by summer 2026. The bilateral cooperation framework signals ongoing regulatory alignment between two major financial centers, which affects cross-border trading, clearing, and settlement operations.
macro_context: The Working Group was established in 2018 to deepen bilateral regulatory cooperation between the UK and US, promoting economic growth, financial stability, investor protection, and capital formation. This 12th meeting continues the biannual dialogue. Key forward items: TTMF recommendations (summer 2026), UK T+1 transition (October 2027), and ongoing stablecoin/payments modernization work.
market_structure_context: UK committed to T+1 settlement cycle by October 2027 (previously T+2). TTMF exploring cross-border capital market links and digital assets. Working Group covers NBFI, operational resilience, critical third-party dependencies, and AI in financial services.
